title: Add submission: 10L Slide64 Mid6, val_bpb=1.1732
body:
This PR submits an updated valid `10-minute / 8xH100 / <16MB` run from the 10-layer sliding-window family.

Result:
- `final_sliding_window_exact val_bpb: 1.17319477`
- `final_quant_zlib_roundtrip_exact val_bpb: 1.20735381`
- `Total submission size quant+zlib: 15846677 bytes`

What changed relative to the earlier valid `mid6` submission in this PR:
- kept the same `10L` architecture
- kept sliding-window eval at `1024/64`
- kept fp16 tied-embedding export
- kept the valid mixed export policy with only middle blocks `3,4,5,6` at int6
- kept the stronger Muon crossover schedule
- changed only `TIED_EMBED_LR` from `0.10` to `0.08`

Why this variant:
- the earlier valid `mid6` run reached `1.17334285` under the cap
- increasing eval context to `2048` did not help and badly regressed the sliding metric
- a smaller tied-embedding LR gave a measurable score improvement while also shrinking the final compressed artifact slightly

Files included in the submission folder:
- `README.md`
- `submission.json`
- `train.log`
- `train_gpt.py`

Quick validation:
```bash
ls records/track_10min_16mb/2026-03-20_10L_Slide64_Mid6
python3 -m py_compile records/track_10min_16mb/2026-03-20_10L_Slide64_Mid6/train_gpt.py
rg -n "Total submission size quant\\+zlib|final_quant_zlib_roundtrip_exact|final_sliding_window_exact" \
  records/track_10min_16mb/2026-03-20_10L_Slide64_Mid6/train.log
cat records/track_10min_16mb/2026-03-20_10L_Slide64_Mid6/submission.json
```
